reasoning: The informal statement "all the roots of x^m - x are distinct" is the standard way to express that the polynomial is separable (has no repeated roots). The corrected formalization captures this via `.Separable`. The original instead claims `card (rootSet ... F) = m`, i.e., that F itself contains m = p^n distinct roots. This is strictly stronger: having m distinct roots in F of a degree-m polynomial implies separability, but separability does not imply F contains all the roots. The original is provably false in general — for F = 𝔽_p (characteristic p) and n = 2, the polynomial X^(p²) - X has only p roots in 𝔽_p, not p². So original ⟹ corrected (making it "stronger"), but the original as a statement is false. The error is a misreading of the informal claim: interpreting "all roots are distinct" as "F contains m distinct roots" rather than "the polynomial is separable."
